# Ashim Sharma

> Software engineer sharing projects, notes, and guides on ML infrastructure.

## Write-ups

- [Extending Claude Code: Skills, Subagents, and Hooks](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/write-up/claude-code-skills-subagents-and-hooks/index.md): Build your own Claude Code extensions from scratch: skills with SKILL.md, custom subagents, and lifecycle hooks. Copy-paste examples, a combined worked workflow, and a tour of the most popular skill repos on GitHub.
- [The Riemann Hypothesis](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/write-up/riemann-zeta-and-the-riemann-hypothesis/index.md): Why the zeros of the zeta function govern the primes, built from scratch with live graphs you can zoom, drag, and animate.
- [MLOps Tooling](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/write-up/mlops-tooling-from-experiment-tracking-to-production/index.md): A conceptual guide to MLOps tooling — MLflow, artifact versioning, reproducible pipelines, framework integrations, alternatives, and deploying and improving models in production.
- [CPUs, GPUs, and TPUs](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/write-up/cpu-gpu-tpu-hardware-deep-dive/index.md): Why the same matrix multiply runs 100× faster on one chip than another. CPU/GPU/TPU internals, tensor cores, precision, GPU memory and the roofline, interconnects, and when to use each. Visual-first.
- [The Linux & Unix Systems Handbook](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/write-up/linux-systems-handbook/index.md): Hands-on handbook covering the Linux filesystem, shell, processes, storage, networking, and system hardening — built around the core models behind every production problem.
- [Engineering the Agentic Harness](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/write-up/harness-context-loop-engineering/index.md): A practical field guide to the systems that wrap an LLM and turn it into a reliable agent — with patterns, worked examples, real deployments, and where the field is heading (mid-2026).
- [Docker Fundamentals](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/write-up/docker-fundamentals/index.md): Docker from first principles — runtime stack, OverlayFS, bridge networking, security, and kernel internals.
- [Anyscale and Ray: A Deep Dive into Distributed AI Compute](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/write-up/anyscale-ray-deep-dive/index.md): A comprehensive technical writeup covering why distributed computing for AI is hard, what Ray is, what Anyscale adds on top of it, how it compares to the alternatives, and how to actually use it.
- [From Raw Data to ML-Ready: A Pandas Walkthrough](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/write-up/from-raw-data-to-ml-ready-a-pandas-walkthrough/index.md): Full ML data prep lifecycle in Pandas: load, clean, impute, engineer features, encode, scale, and ship to model.
- [ML Engineer: Comprehensive Technical Prep Guide](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/write-up/ml-engineer-comprehensive-technical-prep-guide/index.md): ML engineer prep: Python infra, NumPy, PyTorch, distributed training, scientific formats, MLflow, and common coding patterns.
- [PyTorch: From First Tensor to Distributed Training](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/write-up/pytorch-from-first-tensor-to-distributed-training/index.md): Deep guide for ML engineers: tensors, autograd, nn.Module, training loops, DDP, FSDP, checkpointing, and debugging at scale.

## About

- [Profile](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/profile.md): Bio, skills, experience, contact

## Optional

- [Full site content](https://ashimsharma10.github.io/llms-full.txt): All posts concatenated
